tasks.title: Learning contract: learning-contract.md + learning-schema.json
tasks.description: Create the two contract files that anchor the learning capture system. learning-schema.json (schema_version '1', jsonschema draft-07) declares entry fields: schema_version (const '1'), skill (string), timestamp (ISO 8601 string), trigger (enum: tool_failure | backtrack | user_correction | instruction_gap | redundant_effort | uncategorized), trigger_label (string|null — required non-null only when trigger == uncategorized, must be null for all other trigger values; enforce with if/then/else), evidence (string), learning (string), suggested_fix (string|null). timestamp and schema_version are injected server-side by log-learning.py, so they are required in the output schema but NOT required fields that the skill must supply in its candidate input. learning-contract.md documents the schema, the full producers list (all 24 shared skills by name), consumer (future improve-skill), the open-vocabulary trigger rule (grounding not enum-match is the gate; recurring uncategorized labels get promoted to named triggers), and the enumerate-discrete-anchors rule (for aggregated observations, evidence must list discrete quoted transcript anchors rather than asserting a bare count). Place both files under claude-code-shared/contracts/.
acceptance_criteria: learning-schema.json validates sample entries using `python -m jsonschema`; valid entries pass, entries missing required fields or with invalid trigger values fail

trigger_label constraint: an entry with trigger='uncategorized' and trigger_label=null fails validation; an entry with trigger='tool_failure' and trigger_label='something' fails validation; an entry with trigger='uncategorized' and trigger_label='repeated_glob_search' passes
learning-contract.md names all 24 shared skill producers explicitly
learning-contract.md states the enumerate-discrete-anchors rule
learning-contract.md lists future improve-skill as the consumer

tasks.title: log-learning.py: shared validating JSONL writer
tasks.description: Create claude-code-shared/scripts/log-learning.py. The script reads a candidate learning entry as JSON from stdin (the skill supplies trigger, trigger_label, evidence, learning, suggested_fix, and skill name). The script injects timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC now) and schema_version '1' server-side, validates the resulting object against learning-schema.json using the existing validate-schema.py machinery (jsonschema dependency already in the repo), then appends it atomically to claude-code-shared/learnings/<skill>.jsonl. On first write, create the learnings/ directory and the per-skill file. Atomic append strategy: acquire an exclusive lock (fcntl.flock or equivalent), append the JSON line, flush and release. Exit 0 on success. Exit non-zero and print a descriptive message to stderr on schema validation failure, missing skill field, or write error. Never write a partial or invalid entry. A deterministic whitespace-normalized substring pre-filter is optional (cheap sanity check before spawning the judge) but the schema validation is the hard gate.
acceptance_criteria: Script exits 0 and appends a valid JSONL line when given a well-formed entry (minus timestamp/schema_version) on stdin
Script exits non-zero and prints to stderr when given an entry that fails learning-schema.json validation (e.g. missing evidence field)
Script creates the learnings/ directory and <skill>.jsonl file on first call for a new skill
The appended entry contains an injected ISO 8601 timestamp and schema_version '1'
Running the script twice with the same input appends two lines (idempotency is the caller's concern)

tasks.title: learning-grounding-judge agent
tasks.description: Create claude-code-shared/agents/learning-grounding-judge.md. A dedicated single-purpose haiku agent (set `model: haiku` in frontmatter). Given a candidate learning entry (JSON, included in the prompt) and the path to the live session transcript, the agent verifies that each anchor quoted in the entry's evidence field actually appears (verbatim or near-verbatim) in the transcript. The agent does NOT free-discover learnings: the skill enumerates discrete transcript anchors in the evidence field, and the judge confirms each exists. The agent returns a JSON object only: {"grounded": true|false, "reason": "<explanation>"}. grounded=true only when every cited anchor is confirmed present in the transcript. Missing or unconfirmed anchors → grounded=false with a reason that names the specific missing anchor. Include the enumerate-discrete-anchors rule in the agent's instructions: evidence must list quoted anchors, not bare counts; len(anchors) is the count. Model the file structure on existing agents in claude-code-shared/agents/ (frontmatter + description + instructions sections). Add an entry to claude-code-shared/agents/registry.json.
acceptance_criteria: Agent file has `model: haiku` in its YAML frontmatter
Agent instructions define the input contract: candidate entry JSON (inlined in prompt) + transcript file path
Agent instructions define the output contract: JSON {grounded: bool, reason: string} with no surrounding prose
Agent instructions include the enumerate-discrete-anchors rule
An entry is added to claude-code-shared/agents/registry.json for learning-grounding-judge

tasks.title: Managed tail block + inject-learning-tail.py, applied to all 24 shared skills
tasks.description:
Write the canonical managed tail block and the inject-learning-tail.py script that installs or replaces it in every shared SKILL.md, following the sync-model-tiers.py pattern.

The tail block is a Markdown section delimited by sentinel comments (<!-- learning-capture:start --> and <!-- learning-capture:end -->) appended at the end of each SKILL.md. The block instructs the skill to run the grounding gate as the final step of every run:
1. Assess whether an observable correction-event occurred this run. Default: write nothing. Most runs record nothing.
2. If yes, build a candidate entry: trigger (from the open enum), trigger_label (if trigger == uncategorized), evidence (discrete quoted transcript anchors — no bare counts), learning (the general reusable rule), optional suggested_fix.
3. Spawn learning-grounding-judge with the entry JSON and the live session transcript path.
4. If grounded=true: echo the entry JSON to stdin of `python ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/log-learning.py`.
5. If grounded=false or no correction-event: write nothing.

inject-learning-tail.py reads the sentinel block from a canonical source string in the script itself, iterates over all SKILL.md files under claude-code-shared/skills/*/, finds the sentinel block (or end of file), replaces or appends it, and writes back. Exclude plugin skill directories (any directory not directly under claude-code-shared/skills/). Run the script after writing it to apply the block to all 24 skills.
acceptance_criteria: inject-learning-tail.py exists under claude-code-shared/scripts/ and exits 0 when run against the skills directory
All 24 shared SKILL.md files contain the sentinel-delimited tail block at end of file after running the script
Re-running the script is idempotent: a second run makes no changes to any file
The tail block references log-learning.py and learning-grounding-judge by their correct absolute paths
Plugin skill directories (caveman, chrome-devtools) are not modified

tasks.title: register-skill: stamp tail block and producer entry into new skills
tasks.description: Update claude-code-shared/skills/register-skill/SKILL.md so that when a new shared skill is registered it automatically receives: (1) the learning capture tail block — run `python ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/inject-learning-tail.py` on the new skill's directory or copy the sentinel-delimited block directly; (2) the new skill name added to the producers list in claude-code-shared/contracts/learning-contract.md. The register-skill flow already stamps other boilerplate into new skills; these two steps extend that existing pattern. No changes to log-learning.py or learning-schema.json are needed.
acceptance_criteria: register-skill SKILL.md includes a step to append the sentinel-delimited tail block to a new skill's SKILL.md (by running the injector or copying the block)
register-skill SKILL.md includes a step to add the new skill name to the producers list in learning-contract.md
Both steps reference the correct file paths
